---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Yingjie"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---



# Setup

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

rm(list = ls())

library(readr)
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
require(scales) # to access break formatting functions

ymd <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d"); ymd


dir.g <- 'G:/Shared drives' # on Windows
dir.g <- '/Users/yingjiel/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-yingjiel@stanford.edu/Shared drives' # on Mac

dir.desc  <- './data/data_desc'
dir.batch <- file.path(dir.g, 'invest-health/City')
dir.500   <- file.path(dir.g, 'invest-health/City500')
```



# Data



```{r - pop}

dir.aois  <- file.path(dir.g, "invest-health/shp/places_in_or_touching_metros_487.shp")

aois <- st_read(dir.aois) %>% st_drop_geometry()

```


```{r - model output}

f <- file.path(dir.500, 'Cost_NDVI_TCCAdjusted_Monotonic_V1/pct10/PD_Results/results_with_pd_v1_pct10_a_20260623_154121.csv')
prev_case <- read_csv(f, show_col_types = FALSE)

length(unique(prev_case$city))
```



```{r - merge data}

dat <- prev_case %>%
  mutate(
    pd_case_million     = pd_cases / 1e6,
    pd_cost_million_usd = health_cost_savings_usd / 1e6,
  )
```




### US-500

```{r - helper data}

library(sf)
library(ggplot2)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(dplyr)

# --- 1. MOCK DATA CREATION (Replace with your actual CSV loading) ---
# If loading from file: df_city <- read.csv("your_data.csv")


# --- 2. PREPARE THE BASEMAP ---
# Download US state boundaries
us_states <- ne_states(country = "united states of america", returnclass = "sf")

# Filter out Alaska and Hawaii for a cleaner "Lower 48" view
# (EPSG:5070 distorts them if not moved using specialized functions)
us_mainland <- us_states %>% 
  filter(!name %in% c("Alaska", "Hawaii"))

# Reproject basemap to US Albers Equal Area (EPSG:5070)
us_mainland <- st_transform(us_mainland, crs = 5070)

```


```{r fitting}

library(ggplot2)

# Create a reusable function
plot_histogram <- function(data, variable, bins = 15, var_label = NULL) {
  
  # Use variable name if label not provided
  if (is.null(var_label)) var_label <- variable
  
  # Calculate mean
  mean_val <- mean(data[[variable]], na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Create plot
  ggplot(data, aes(x = .data[[variable]])) +
    geom_histogram(bins = bins, 
                   fill = "#69b3a2", 
                   color = "white",
                   alpha = 0.8) +
    geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mean_val),
               color = "red", 
               linetype = "dashed", 
               linewidth = 1) +
    annotate("text", 
             x = mean_val, 
             y = Inf, 
             label = paste("Mean =", round(mean_val, 2)),
             vjust = 1.5, 
             hjust = -0.1,
             color = "red",
             size = 4) +
    labs(x = var_label,
         y = "Count") +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
}


# Use the function
p_tc_0  <- plot_histogram(dat, "current_tcc",   bins = 15, "TC Mean")
p_ndvi <- plot_histogram(dat, "observed_ndvi", bins = 15, "NDVI Mean")

p_tc_tar <- plot_histogram(dat, variable = "target_tcc",  bins = 30, var_label = "target_tcc")
p_tc_new <- plot_histogram(dat, variable = "new_tcc_pct", bins = 20, var_label = "new_tcc_pct")

library(ggpubr)
ggarrange(p_tc_0, p_ndvi, p_tc_tar, p_tc_new, nrow = 2, ncol = 2)
```


# Viz

```{r - plot}

library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

dat %>%
  filter(city == '0412000') %>%
  ggplot(., aes(x = current_tcc, 
                y = pd_case_million, 
                # color = treecover_gap,
                size = actual_delta_tcc, 
                label = city)) +
  geom_point(
    color = "orange",
    # size = 3
    # shape = 1,  # open circle
    # shape = 19, # filled circle
    shape = 16, 
    alpha = .7
    ) +
  # geom_text(vjust = -1, size = 3, show.legend = F) +  # labels slightly above points
  geom_text_repel(size = 3, 
                  box.padding = 0.6,   # space around text
                  point.padding = 0.2, # space around points
                  segment.color = "grey80",  # color of line connecting label to point
                  max.overlaps = Inf, 
                  show.legend = F) +
  # scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue") +
  scale_size_binned(nice.breaks = T) +
  theme_minimal() +
  guides(
    size = guide_legend(title = "Treecover Gap"),
    color = guide_legend(title = "Treecover Gap")) +
  # coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = "Tree Cover Mean (%)",
    y = "Total preventable cases (in millions)",
    color = "Treecover Gap",
    title = "Tree Cover vs Total Preventable Cases"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.box.background = element_rect(colour = "gray", fill = "gray95"), 
    legend.position = c(0.9, 0.15))

f <- paste0('./figures/pd_vs_tcc_', ymd, '.png')
ggsave(f, width = 8, height = 6, units = 'in', dpi = 300)
```




```{r load point shp}

f <- file.path(dir.g, 'invest-health', 'shp', 'aoi487_polylabel.gpkg')

pt <- st_read(f) 

names(pt)


pt <- pt %>%
  select(-any_of(c("POP2020", "POP2021", "POP2022", "POP2023", "POP2024")))
# plot(pt[1])
```


```{r join}

tcc_inc <- 10

df_batch <- dat %>%
  dplyr::filter(tcc_increase_pct == tcc_inc) 


df_city <- dat %>%
  group_by(city) %>%
  summarise_at(vars(pd_cases), sum, na.rm = TRUE) %>%
  
  ## add sf
  left_join(x = pt,
            y = ., 
            by = c('GEOID_PLAC' = 'city')
            ) %>%
  rename('POP2021_city' = 'POP2021_le') %>%
  mutate(pd_per1kpop = round(pd_cases / POP2021_city * 1000, digits = 2))


```


```{r hist}


# --- 3. PREPARE YOUR CITY DATA ---
# Convert dataframe to simple feature object (sf)
# Note: coords expects c("Longitude", "Latitude") order!
# cities_sf <- st_as_sf(df_city, coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326)
cities_sf <- df_city

hist(df_city$pd_per1kpop)
```


```{r map}
# Reproject cities to match the basemap
cities_sf <- st_transform(cities_sf, crs = 5070)

# --- 4. PLOT WITH GGPLOT2 ---
ggplot() +
  # Layer A: Base Map
  geom_sf(data = us_mainland, 
          fill = "gray90", 
          color = "white", 
          size = 0.2) +
  
  # Layer B: City Points
  geom_sf(data = cities_sf, 
          aes(size = pd_per1kpop, 
              fill = pd_per1kpop), 
          shape = 21, 
          color = "black",
          stroke = 0.5,
          alpha = 0.5) +
  
  # --- 5. STYLING ---
  # # Color palette (Magma is high contrast and colorblind friendly)
  # scale_color_viridis_c(option = "magma", 
  #                       name = "Dead Cases",
  #                       direction = -1) + # Darker = more cases
  
  # Size scaling
  # scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 8), name = "Dead Cases") +
  
  # --- IMPROVEMENT A: SIZE SCALING ---
  # scale_size_area ensures 0 = size 0. max_size=12 makes the '8' big enough to see.
  # breaks defines what shows in the legend.
  scale_size_area(max_size = 8, breaks = c(1, 2, 4, 8, 11), name = 'pd_per1k') +
  
  
  # --- IMPROVEMENT B: BINNED COLORS ---
  # Using a binned scale ('viridis_b') makes distinct color steps
  # distinct values are easier to read than a smooth gradient
  scale_fill_viridis_b(option = "magma", direction = -1, breaks = c(1, 2, 4, 8, 11), name = 'pd_per1k') +
  
  # Labels and Theme
  labs(title = "US Cities: Distribution of Dead Cases",
       subtitle = "Projected in US Albers Equal Area (EPSG:5070)") +
  
  
  # Guide customization to merge size/fill into one clean legend
  # guides(
  #   size = guide_legend(title = "Dead Cases"),
  #   fill = guide_legend(title = "Dead Cases")
  # ) +
  
  theme_void() + # Removes axes and gridlines for a clean map look
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, color = "gray50")
  )
```
